Product Ideas - Brain Dump
=========================

The whole point of this thing is to make project management less painful for small teams (5-20 people). Current tools are either too simple (sticky notes, spreadsheets) or too complex (Jira, Monday).

Key insight: most small teams don't need Gantt charts or sprint planning. They need:
- A clear list of what needs to be done
- Who's responsible
- What's blocked and why
- A simple way to communicate progress

Feature thoughts:

PROJECTS
- Each project has a name, description, due date, and owner
- Projects contain tasks
- A project can be "active", "completed", or "archived"
- Users should be able to star/favorite projects for quick access

TASKS
- Tasks belong to a project
- Each task has: title, description, assignee, status (todo/in-progress/done/blocked), priority (low/medium/high/urgent)
- Tasks can have comments for async discussion
- File attachments on tasks would be nice but maybe v2?
- Subtasks? Maybe. Keep it simple for now.

NOTIFICATIONS
- When you're assigned a task, you should know
- When a task you're watching changes status, you should know
- Daily digest option — "here's what changed in your projects today"
- Don't over-notify. Nobody likes notification spam.

SEARCH
- Global search across projects and tasks
- Filter by assignee, status, project, date range
- Full-text search in task descriptions and comments

What we DON'T want:
- No time tracking (use Toggl or whatever)
- No billing/invoicing
- No complex workflow automation
- No Gantt charts (seriously)

Competitive edge: simplicity. If a feature doesn't make the tool simpler or more useful for a 10-person team, we don't build it.
